title: Repository Guidelines
text: R/ holds core functions S3 methods (example model_fit, compare_model_fit, print.*) across multiple model classes. man/ generated Rd documentation; man/figures/ stores README assets like hex logo. inst/templates/ contains APA-styled Word templates used save_table(). README.Rmd source README.md; NEWS.md tracks user-facing changes; DESCRIPTION NAMESPACE define package metadata.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: R CMD build . — build source tarball. R CMD check <tarball> — run full package checks built tarball (preferred checking source tree). tarball check returns Status: OK instructions, remove tarball <pkg>.Rcheck directory; check end OK, leave inspection. R -q -e "roxygen2::roxygenise()" — regenerate man/ NAMESPACE doc changes. R -q -e "rmarkdown::render('README.Rmd')" — refresh README.md Rmd source.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Use two-space indentation snake_case functions variables. Document exported functions roxygen2 (@param, @return, @export). hand-edit generated files (NAMESPACE, man/*.Rd).

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Ensure user-facing messages honor function’s verbose flag present. compare_model_fit() messaging, prefer including affected model name(s) clarity.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: testthat suite exists tests/testthat; coverage expanding exhaustive. Use R -q -e "devtools::test()" quick feedback, rely R CMD check plus runnable examples release readiness. roadmap tracks continued test expansion R CMD check cleanup; see ROADMAP.md scope. adding tests, follow testthat conventions (tests/testthat/test-*.R) add testthat Suggests. tests/examples involving fit indices, avoid 1-factor/3-item model (df = 0 gives identical indices). Use least 1 factor 4 items 2 factors 3 items .

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Keep mandatory dependencies minimal; integrations live Suggests (lavaan, ggplot2, flextable, officer). optional model classes, guard usage rlang::check_installed() provide clear messages instead hard dependencies. Assume users already package produced model objects; avoid forcing installs unrelated workflows.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: ROADMAP.md source truth milestones; GitHub issues linked contain detailed specs. Near-term priorities include SEM support lavaan, new estimate helpers, invariance tooling, expanding model-class support (mirt, psych).

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Table pipeline: prepare_table() (internal) builds data.frame insight::format_table(); format_results() (public) returns text/markdown/html/auto; print.* console-accepts ... S3 consistency. File structure (rule thumb): model_fit-<backend>.R model_fit.<external_class>() backend helpers; model_fit-methods.R model_fit result methods; compare_model_fit.R main function; compare_model_fit-methods.R compare_model_fit result methods; format_results.R prepare_table.R generics shared helpers; helpers-formatting.R internal formatting helpers. Backend checklist: 1) create R/model_fit-<backend>.R; 2) implement model_fit.<class>(); 3) ensure return compatible result methods (print/format_results/prepare_table).

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Commit history uses short, imperative messages common prefixes like feat:, fix:, docs:, chore:. PRs include brief summary, tests run (note run), updates NEWS.md user-visible changes. asked verify recent changes, use git log --oneline --left-right --graph --decorate origin/master...gitbutler/workspace non-destructive history/diff commands needed. agent create commits PRs (GitButler workflow). requested release prep, assist entries NEWS.md.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: writing complex features significant refactors, use ExecPlan (described .agent/PLANS.md) design implementation.

title: Development Roadmap & Versioning Plan
text: document outlines development plan versioning strategy psymetrics package. serves portable, high-level guide development priorities. file contains overall plan, day--day discussion detailed technical specifications task live linked GitHub Issues.

title: Development Roadmap & Versioning Plan
text: 🚧 Progress: v0.4.0 - Implement compare_model_estimates() Current milestone work focused parameter-level comparisons across fitted models. Add compare_model_estimates() comparing parameter estimates across two models. Provide output compatible existing formatting export workflows (print(), format_results(), save_table()). Complete acceptance criteria tracked Issue #18. ✅ Completed: v0.3.0 - Implement model_estimates() Added model_estimates() support lavaan CFA/SEM workflows. Integrated model_estimates formatting DOCX export pipelines. Expanded tests documentation extraction, formatting, export behavior. Completed milestone acceptance criteria tracked Issue #17 (implemented via PR #44, merged February 25, 2026). ✅ Completed: v0.2.0 - Extend Existing Functions SEM Officially validated documented SEM support fitted lavaan objects model_fit(), compare_model_fit(), plot_factor_loadings(). Expanded SEM coverage structural models, growth models, multigroup/multilevel contexts, categorical estimators, missing-data handling, non-converged/path-edge cases. Completed milestone acceptance criteria tracked Issue #25. ✅ Completed: v0.1.9.x - Stability & Documentation Polish Expanded testthat coverage core workflows edge cases. Improved formatting/messaging behavior aligned documentation current implementation. ✅ Completed: v0.1.5 - Stability & Testing Release Added initial testthat suite covering model_fit, compare_model_fit, plotting, save_table.

what: Phase 1: Complete and Consolidate lavaan Workflow (CFA & SEM)
title: Development Roadmap & Versioning Plan
text: focus phase build robust feature-complete workflow models fitted lavaan, covering Confirmatory Factor Analysis (CFA) general Structural Equation Models (SEM). v0.2.0: Extend Existing Functions SEM. (Completed February 8, 2026) Goal: Officially support SEM model_fit, compare_model_fit, plot_factor_loadings. Details: Issue #25 v0.3.0: Implement model_estimates() parameter extraction. (Completed February 25, 2026) Details: Issue #17 v0.4.0: Implement compare_model_estimates() parameter comparison. Details: Issue #18 v0.5.0: Implement plot_model_fit() visualizing fit indices. Details: Issue #19 v0.6.0: Enhance compare_model_fit() Measurement Invariance (MG-CFA). Details: Issue #20 v0.7.0: Create helper function specifying invariance models. Details: Issue #21 v0.8.0: Add Excel (.xlsx) export functionality save_table(). Details: Issue #22

what: Phase 2: Expansion to Other Models & Packages
title: Development Roadmap & Versioning Plan
text: phase commence core lavaan functionality stable complete. v0.9.0: Add full support lavaan EFA models. Goal: Extend relevant functions compatible Exploratory Factor Analysis models fitted lavaan. v0.10.0: Add full support mirt objects. Goal: Extend functions compatible models mirt package. v0.11.0: Add full support psych EFA objects. Goal: Extend functions compatible EFA models psych package.

title: Development Roadmap & Versioning Plan
text: v1.0.0: Stable & Complete Release. Goal: features implemented, documented, thoroughly tested. package API considered reliable stable. Comprehensive documentation, including tutorials (vignettes), complete.

what: Step 1: Fit a baseline CFA model
title: Get started with fit indices
code: model <- '   visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3   textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6   speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9 '  fit_mlr <- cfa(   model,   data = HolzingerSwineford1939,   estimator = "MLR" )

title: Get started with fit indices
text: Request selected metrics needed:

title: Get started with fit indices

what: Step 4: Ordinal example with WLSMV (ordered = TRUE)
title: Get started with fit indices
text: example converts nine Holzinger-Swineford items (x1 x9) four ordered categories, fits CFA model WLSMV.
code: item_names <- paste0("x", 1:9) ordered_data <- HolzingerSwineford1939  ordered_data[item_names] <- lapply(   ordered_data[item_names],   function(x) ordered(cut(x, breaks = 4, include.lowest = TRUE)) )  fit_wlsmv <- cfa(   model,   data = ordered_data,   estimator = "WLSMV",   ordered = TRUE )  model_fit(fit_wlsmv) #> NOBS | ESTIMATOR | NPAR | Chi2(24) | p (Chi2) |  CFI  |  TLI  | RMSEA #> --------------------------------------------------------------------- #> 301  |   WLSMV   |  39  |  63.89   |  < .001  | 0.969 | 0.954 | 0.074 #>  #> NOBS |   RMSEA  CI    | SRMR  #> ----------------------------- #> 301  | [0.053, 0.097] | 0.066 compare_model_fit(MLR = fit_mlr, WLSMV = fit_wlsmv) #> MODEL | NOBS | ESTIMATOR | NPAR | Chi2(24) | p (Chi2) |  CFI  |  TLI  | RMSEA #> ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- #> MLR   | 301  |    MLR    |  21  |  87.13   |  < .001  | 0.925 | 0.888 | 0.093 #> WLSMV | 301  |   WLSMV   |  39  |  63.89   |  < .001  | 0.969 | 0.954 | 0.074 #>  #> MODEL |   RMSEA  CI    | SRMR  #> ------------------------------ #> MLR   | [0.073, 0.115] | 0.065 #> WLSMV | [0.053, 0.097] | 0.066

what: Compare Model Fit Indices Across Multiple Models — compare_model_fit
title: Compare Model Fit Indices Across Multiple Models — compare_model_fit
text: compare_model_fit() compares fit indices two models. extracts fit indices using model_fit() combines single data frame easy comparison. supports fitted lavaan model objects (including CFA, SEM, growth, multigroup, multilevel variants).

title: Compare Model Fit Indices Across Multiple Models — compare_model_fit
code: compare_model_fit(   ...,   type = NULL,   metrics = "essential",   verbose = TRUE,   test = "default",   standard_test = FALSE,   test_details = FALSE )

title: Compare Model Fit Indices Across Multiple Models — compare_model_fit
text: ... Two model objects compared. lavaan, pass fitted objects created lavaan::cfa(), lavaan::sem(), lavaan::growth(), related functions. type character string specifying type fit indices extract. Options "standard", "scaled", "robust". Defaults NULL, automatically selects "scaled" robust estimator used, otherwise "standard". metrics character vector specifying fit indices extract. Defaults "essential", custom vector indices. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints messages indices adjusted. test character string vector specifying lavaan tests report. Use "default" (default) return non-standard tests lavInspect(fit, "options")$test, excluding "standard", "default", "none". Provide character vector request specific tests; unavailable entries dropped informational message verbose = TRUE. supplying list, must named name must match model labels shown MODEL column. supply named arguments, may also reference original object names unambiguous. standard_test logical value indicating whether include standard-test row addition non-standard tests. TRUE, standard row shown first always uses standard indices. supplying list, must named name must match model labels shown MODEL column. supply named arguments, may also reference original object names unambiguous. test_details Logical. TRUE, include TEST SE columns (lavaan fits) describe test standard error settings used compute row. standard_test = TRUE adds standard row, SE set NA row fit indices depend standard errors.

title: Compare Model Fit Indices Across Multiple Models — compare_model_fit
text: data frame containing fit indices model, additional column identifying models.

title: Compare Model Fit Indices Across Multiple Models — compare_model_fit
code: if (requireNamespace("lavaan", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(lavaan)   library(psymetrics)   hs_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3                textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6                speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9'   fit1 <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939, estimator = "ML")   fit2 <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939, estimator = "MLR")   compare_model_fit(fit1, fit2)   sem_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3                 textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6                 speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9                 textual ~ visual                 speed ~ textual'   sem1 <- sem(sem_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939, estimator = "ML")   sem2 <- sem(sem_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939, estimator = "MLR")   compare_model_fit(sem1, sem2)   compare_model_fit(fit1, fit2, standard_test = TRUE, test_details = TRUE)   compare_model_fit(fit1, fit2, metrics = c("cfi", "tli", "rmsea"))   fit3 <- cfa(     hs_model,     data = HolzingerSwineford1939,     test = c("satorra.bentler", "mean.var.adjusted")   )   compare_model_fit(fit1, fit3, test = "satorra.bentler")   compare_model_fit(     fit1 = fit1,     robust = fit3,     test = list(robust = "mean.var.adjusted"),     standard_test = list(robust = TRUE),     test_details = TRUE   ) } else {   message("Please install 'lavaan' to run this example.") } #> This is lavaan 0.6-21 #> lavaan is FREE software! Please report any bugs. #> Standard-test row uses standard indices for estimator ML. Affected model: fit2. #> cfi, tli, and rmsea were adjusted to their scaled version. #> If you want to control the specific metric type used, specify it explicitly #> (e.g., `cfi.robust`) or modify the type argument. #> Requested tests not found in the fit1 model and were dropped: satorra.bentler. #> Standard-test row uses standard indices for estimator ML. Affected model: #> robust. #> MODEL  | NOBS | ESTIMATOR  |       TEST        |    SE    | NPAR | Chi2  #> ------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> fit1   | 301  |     ML     |     standard      | standard |  21  | 85.31 #> robust | 301  |     ML     |     standard      |          |  21  | 85.31 #> robust | 301  | ML_variant | mean.var.adjusted | standard |  21  | 67.23 #>  #> MODEL  | Chi2_df | p (Chi2) |  CFI  |  TLI  | RMSEA |   RMSEA  CI    | SRMR  #> ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- #> fit1   |  24.00  |  < .001  | 0.931 | 0.896 | 0.092 | [0.071, 0.114] | 0.065 #> robust |  24.00  |  < .001  | 0.931 | 0.896 | 0.092 | [0.071, 0.114] | 0.065 #> robust |  19.95  |  < .001  | 0.734 | 0.887 | 0.089 | [0.068, 0.110] | 0.065 #>

what: Format model results for output — format_results
title: Format model results for output — format_results
text: format_results() prepares base table exports text, markdown, HTML. Use output = "auto" choose markdown HTML based rendering context; best-effort heuristic, set output explicitly override.

title: Format model results for output — format_results
code: format_results(   x,   output = c("auto", "text", "markdown", "md", "html"),   digits = 3,   ci_digits = digits,   p_digits = 3,   align = "firstleft",   digits_by_col = NULL,   table_args = list(),   output_args = list() )

title: Format model results for output — format_results
text: x object containing table data. output Output format. One "auto", "text", "markdown", "md", "html". digits integer indicating number decimal places use formatting numeric columns. Defaults 3. ci_digits Number digits rounding confidence intervals. Default digits. p_digits Number digits rounding p-values. Default 3. align Alignment text markdown output (ignored HTML). Passed insight::export_table(). Defaults "firstleft", options like "left", "right", "center", custom specifications "lccrl". See insight::export_table() details. digits_by_col Named integer vector forces digits selected columns. Applied export. model_fit compare_model_fit, defaults c(Chi2 = 2, Chi2_df = 2) supplied. Chi2_df fractional, rounded two decimals forming Chi2(df) header. table_args named list arguments forwarded insight::format_table(). output_args named list arguments forwarded insight::export_table() tinytable::tt() depending output. align supplied top-level align argument missing, output_args$align used. Supplying align places error. output_args$format allowed; use output instead.

title: Format model results for output — format_results
text: character string (text), knitr_kable (markdown), tinytable (HTML).

title: Format model results for output — format_results
text: HTML output returns tinytable object. Printing HTML tables inside RStudio requires rstudioapi package; can still create object without , printing error unless rstudioapi installed.

title: Format model results for output — format_results
code: if (requireNamespace("lavaan", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(lavaan)   library(psymetrics)    hs_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3               textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6               speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9'    fit <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939, estimator = "MLR")   results <- model_fit(fit)   format_results(results, output = "text")   format_results(results, output = "text", digits = 2, p_digits = 4)   format_results(     results,     output = "text",     digits_by_col = c(Chi2 = 2, SRMR = 2)   )   format_results(     results,     output = "text",     table_args = list(ci_brackets = FALSE)   )   if (requireNamespace("knitr", quietly = TRUE)) {     format_results(       results,       output = "markdown",       align = "center",       output_args = list(caption = "Fit indices")     )     format_results(       results,       output = "markdown",       output_args = list(align = "center", caption = "Fit indices")     )   }   html_table <- format_results(results, output = "html")   if (interactive()) {     html_table   } } else {   message("Please install 'lavaan' to run this example.") }

title: Extract Parameter Estimates from a lavaan Model — model_estimates.lavaan
text: fit fitted lavaan object (example lavaan::cfa(), lavaan::sem(), lavaan::growth()). standardized Controls standardized extraction. Accepted values FALSE, TRUE, "std.", "std.lv", "std.nox", plus aliases: "", "latent", "lv", "no_exogenous". ci Confidence level interval columns. Must numeric value (0, 1). component Character vector indicating parameter components keep. Use "" (default) keep available components. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints informative messages non-convergence partially unavailable inferential statistics. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

what: Generic Function to Extract Model Fit Indices — model_fit
title: Generic Function to Extract Model Fit Indices — model_fit
text: model_fit() generic function designed extract fit indices variety statistical model objects. serves unified interface obtaining key fit metrics, can vary depending type model specific method implemented model class. Confirmatory Factor Analysis (CFA) Structural Equation Models (SEM): model_fit.lavaan() Exploratory Factor Analysis (Future): Future methods may include support models psych package. IRT models: Future methods may include support models mirt package.

title: Generic Function to Extract Model Fit Indices — model_fit
code: model_fit(fit, ...)

text: fit model object extract fit indices. class object determines specific method used. example, objects class lavaan use method model_fit.lavaan(). ... Additional arguments passed specific method model class.

title: Generic Function to Extract Model Fit Indices — model_fit
text: data.frame containing fit indices model. specific indices returned depend model type method used.

title: Generic Function to Extract Model Fit Indices — model_fit
text: model_fit() function intended provide consistent interface extracting fit indices various types models. Methods specific classes models implement necessary logic retrieve return relevant fit indices tidy format.

what: Extract Fit Indices from a lavaan Model — model_fit.lavaan
title: Extract Fit Indices from a lavaan Model — model_fit.lavaan
text: model_fit.lavaan extracts fit indices lavaan model object CFA, SEM, growth, multigroup, related workflows. function allows specify type indices extract: "standard", "scaled", "robust". model uses robust estimator specify type = "scaled" type = "robust", corresponding indices returned. type specified, function automatically chooses "scaled" robust estimators "standard" otherwise. model fitted multiple tests, function can return multiple rows (one per non-standard test).

title: Extract Fit Indices from a lavaan Model — model_fit.lavaan
code: # S3 method for class 'lavaan' model_fit(   fit,   type = NULL,   metrics = "essential",   verbose = TRUE,   test = "default",   standard_test = FALSE,   test_details = FALSE,   ... )

title: Extract Fit Indices from a lavaan Model — model_fit.lavaan
text: fit fitted lavaan object (example lavaan::cfa(), lavaan::sem(), lavaan::growth(), related functions). type character string specifying type fit indices extract. Options "standard", "scaled", "robust". Defaults NULL, automatically choose "scaled" robust estimator used; otherwise "standard". metrics character vector specifying fit indices return. default "essential", includes common fit indices. can also specify custom set metrics. verbose logical value indicating whether display informational messages metric adjustments. Defaults TRUE. test character string vector specifying lavaan tests report. Use "default" (default) return non-standard tests lavInspect(fit, "options")$test, excluding "standard", "default", "none". Provide character vector request specific tests; unavailable entries dropped informational message verbose = TRUE. NULL treated "default". standard_test logical value indicating whether include standard-test row addition non-standard tests. TRUE, standard row shown first always uses standard indices. test_details logical value indicating whether add TEST SE columns describing test standard error settings used compute fit measures. standard_test = TRUE adds standard row, SE set NA row fit indices depend standard errors. FALSE (default), ESTIMATOR substitutes test name unknown estimator variants. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Extract Fit Indices from a lavaan Model — model_fit.lavaan
text: data frame containing specified fit indices model. multiple tests reported, data frame can include multiple rows.

title: Extract Fit Indices from a lavaan Model — model_fit.lavaan
code: if (requireNamespace("lavaan", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(lavaan)   library(psymetrics)   hs_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3                textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6                speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9'   fit <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939,              estimator = "MLR")   model_fit(fit)   sem_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3                 textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6                 speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9                 textual ~ visual                 speed ~ textual'   fit_sem <- sem(sem_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939)   model_fit(fit_sem)   model_fit(fit, standard_test = TRUE, test_details = TRUE)   model_fit(fit, type = "robust")   model_fit(fit, metrics = c("cfi", "tli"))   fit_test <- cfa(     hs_model,     data = HolzingerSwineford1939,     test = c("satorra.bentler", "mean.var.adjusted")   )   model_fit(fit_test, test = "satorra.bentler") } else {   message("Please install 'lavaan' to run this example.") } #> Standard-test row uses standard indices for estimator ML. #> cfi and tli were adjusted to their scaled version. #> If you want to control the specific metric type used, specify it explicitly #> (e.g., `cfi.robust`) or modify the type argument. #> NOBS |    ESTIMATOR    | NPAR | Chi2(24) | p (Chi2) |  CFI  |  TLI  | RMSEA #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #> 301  | satorra.bentler |  21  |  80.87   |  < .001  | 0.925 | 0.887 | 0.089 #>  #> NOBS |   RMSEA  CI    | SRMR  #> ----------------------------- #> 301  | [0.068, 0.110] | 0.065 #>

what: Generic Plot Function for Model Visualizations — plot-methods

title: Generic Plot Function for Model Visualizations — plot-methods
text: plot object, usually ggplot, specific model type.

title: Generic Plot Function for Model Visualizations — plot-methods
text: Current available method: lavaan CFA SEM models: plot.lavaan() plotting factor loadings metrics models lavaan package. Future methods may include support : Exploratory Factor Analysis: Models psych package. Item Response Theory (IRT): Models mirt package.

what: Plot Method for lavaan Objects — plot.lavaan
title: Plot Method for lavaan Objects — plot.lavaan
text: plot method fitted lavaan objects, including CFA SEM workflows created lavaan::cfa(), lavaan::sem(), related functions. allows users create different types plots visualize key aspects lavaan models, including factor loadings, residuals, path diagrams.

title: Plot Method for lavaan Objects — plot.lavaan
code: # S3 method for class 'lavaan' plot(x, type = "factor_loadings", standardized = TRUE, ci = TRUE, ...)

title: Plot Method for lavaan Objects — plot.lavaan
text: x fitted lavaan model object (example lavaan::cfa(), lavaan::sem(), lavaan::growth()). type character string indicating type plot generate. Options : "factor_loadings": Generates dot plot standardized factor loadings (=~ parameters), optionally including confidence intervals. model measurement component, error raised. "residuals": Generates residual plot visualize differences observed model-implied covariances. "path": Generates path diagram representing model structure, showing relationships latent observed variables. standardized Logical; TRUE, uses standardized estimates factor loadings. applicable type = "factor_loadings". Defaults TRUE. ci Logical; TRUE, includes confidence intervals factor loading plot. applicable type = "factor_loadings". Defaults TRUE. Also accepts CI via ... backward compatibility. ... Additional arguments passed specific plotting functions.

title: Plot Method for lavaan Objects — plot.lavaan
text: ggplot object factor_loadings residuals plots ggplot2 installed, semPlot diagram object path plots. error message returned required packages available.

title: Plot Method for lavaan Objects — plot.lavaan
text: Factor Loadings Plot: Displays dot plot factor loadings (=~ parameters ), items y-axis loadings x-axis. Confidence intervals can added desired. Residuals Plot: Shows residuals (differences observed model-implied covariances), typically heatmap scatterplot. Path Diagram: Illustrates structure model, showing latent variables, observed variables, estimated relationships .

title: Plot Method for lavaan Objects — plot.lavaan
code: if (requireNamespace("lavaan", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(lavaan)   library(psymetrics)   hs_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3                textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6                speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9'   fit <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939, estimator = "MLR")   plot(fit) } else {   message("Please install 'lavaan' and 'ggplot2' to run this example.") }

what: Plot Factor Loadings for lavaan Models — plot_factor_loadings
title: Plot Factor Loadings for lavaan Models — plot_factor_loadings
text: Creates dot plot standardized factor loadings fitted lavaan model, including CFA SEM. plot uses measurement model portion (=~ parameters), along optional confidence intervals item. model contain =~ parameters, function errors clear message.

title: Plot Factor Loadings for lavaan Models — plot_factor_loadings
code: plot_factor_loadings(   fit,   sort = TRUE,   group_by = TRUE,   standardized = TRUE,   ci = TRUE,   autofit = TRUE,   ci_bounds = c("extend", "arrow"),   facet_by = c("none", "group", "level", "group_level"),   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot Factor Loadings for lavaan Models — plot_factor_loadings
text: fit fitted lavaan model object (including CFA SEM). sort Logical; TRUE, sorts items loading size. Defaults TRUE. group_by Logical; TRUE model multiple factors, groups items factor. Defaults TRUE. standardized Logical; TRUE, uses standardized loadings. Defaults TRUE. ci Logical; TRUE, includes confidence intervals. Defaults TRUE. Also accepts CI via ... backward compatibility. autofit Logical; TRUE, computes applies x-axis limits via coord_cartesian() based loadings (CIs available). values non-negative, lower limit set 0; values non-positive, upper limit set 0. standardized loadings within [-1, 1], limits extended include nearest boundary keep standardized scale visible. FALSE, x-axis limits modified ggplot2 determines range. ci_bounds Controls confidence intervals handled standardized loadings ci = TRUE autofit = TRUE. "extend" draws full CIs (axis may extend beyond [-1, 1]); "arrow" constrains x-axis [-1, 1] ([0, 1]/[-1, 0] values non-negative/non-positive), clips CIs range, adds arrows indicate -scale intervals. standardized point estimate outside [-1, 1], "arrow" falls back "extend". facet_by Character string controlling faceting SEM contexts grouping metadata. Options: "none" (default): faceting. "group": facet lavaan group. "level": facet lavaan level (multilevel models). "group_level": facet combined group level. requested metadata unavailable, message shown verbose = TRUE plotting continues without faceting. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints informational messages warnings (example, model converge). Defaults TRUE. FALSE, non-fatal messages warnings internal lavaan extraction calls suppressed; errors still raised. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::ggplot.

title: Plot Factor Loadings for lavaan Models — plot_factor_loadings
text: ggplot object ggplot2 installed, otherwise error message.

title: Plot Factor Loadings for lavaan Models — plot_factor_loadings
code: if (requireNamespace("lavaan", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(lavaan)   library(psymetrics)   hs_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3               textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6               speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9'   fit <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939, estimator = "MLR")   plot_factor_loadings(fit)   sem_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3                 textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6                 speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9                 textual ~ visual                 speed ~ textual'   fit_sem <- sem(sem_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939, group = "school")   plot_factor_loadings(fit_sem, facet_by = "group") } else {   message("Please install 'lavaan' and 'ggplot2' to run this example.") }

what: Print Method for compare_model_fit Objects — print.compare_model_fit
title: Print Method for compare_model_fit Objects — print.compare_model_fit
text: print.compare_model_fit() prints text summary compare-model-fit tables console. default, Chi2 shown 2 decimals indices use digits, fractional chi-square df values rounded 2 decimals Chi2(df) header. markdown HTML output (override column-specific digits), use format_results().

title: Print Method for compare_model_fit Objects — print.compare_model_fit
code: # S3 method for class 'compare_model_fit' print(x, digits = 3, ci_digits = digits, p_digits = 3, ...)

title: Print Method for compare_model_fit Objects — print.compare_model_fit
text: x object class compare_model_fit, typically created compare_model_fit function. digits Number digits rounding numeric values. Default 3. ci_digits Number digits rounding confidence intervals. Default digits. p_digits Number digits rounding p-values. Default 3. ... arguments ignored.

title: Print Method for compare_model_fit Objects — print.compare_model_fit
text: Returns x invisibly.

title: Print Method for compare_model_fit Objects — print.compare_model_fit
code: if (requireNamespace("lavaan", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(psymetrics)   library(lavaan)    hs_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3                textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6                speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9'   fit1 <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939,               estimator = "ML")   fit2 <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939,               estimator = "MLR")   comparison <- compare_model_fit(fit1, fit2)   comparison   print(comparison, digits = 4)   html_table <- format_results(comparison, output = "html")   if (interactive()) {     html_table   } } else {   message("Please install 'lavaan' to run this example.") } #> MODEL | NOBS | ESTIMATOR | NPAR | Chi2(24) | p (Chi2) |  CFI   |  TLI   #> ----------------------------------------------------------------------- #> fit1  | 301  |    ML     |  21  |  85.31   |  < .001  | 0.9306 | 0.8958 #> fit2  | 301  |    MLR    |  21  |  87.13   |  < .001  | 0.9252 | 0.8878 #>  #> MODEL | RMSEA  |    RMSEA  CI     |  SRMR  #> ------------------------------------------ #> fit1  | 0.0921 | [0.0714, 0.1137] | 0.0652 #> fit2  | 0.0935 | [0.0726, 0.1152] | 0.0652 #>

title: Print Method for Model Fit Indices — print.model_fit
code: if (requireNamespace("lavaan", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(lavaan)   library(psymetrics)    hs_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3                textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6                speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9'    fit <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939,              estimator = "MLR")   result <- model_fit(fit)   result   print(result, digits = 4)   if (requireNamespace("knitr", quietly = TRUE)) {     format_results(result, output = "markdown")   } } else {   message("Please install 'lavaan' to run this example.") } #> NOBS | ESTIMATOR | NPAR | Chi2(24) | p (Chi2) |  CFI   |  TLI   | RMSEA  #> ------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> 301  |    MLR    |  21  |  87.13   |  < .001  | 0.9252 | 0.8878 | 0.0935 #>  #> NOBS |    RMSEA  CI     |  SRMR  #> -------------------------------- #> 301  | [0.0726, 0.1152] | 0.0652 #>  #>  #>  #> |NOBS | ESTIMATOR | NPAR | Chi2(24) | p (Chi2) |   CFI |   TLI | RMSEA |      RMSEA  CI |  SRMR | #> |:----|:---------:|:----:|:--------:|:--------:|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:--------------:|:-----:| #> |301  |       MLR |   21 |    87.13 |   < .001 | 0.925 | 0.888 | 0.093 | [0.073, 0.115] | 0.065 |

what: Save a Table to a Specified Format — save_table
title: Save a Table to a Specified Format — save_table
text: save_table() exports table various formats, current support Word documents (.docx). table formatted using specified default template can customized different font styles, sizes, alignment options.

title: Save a Table to a Specified Format — save_table
code: save_table(   table_data,   path,   orientation = "landscape",   template = NULL,   digits = 3,   ci_digits = digits,   p_digits = 3,   digits_by_col = NULL,   table_args = list() )

title: Save a Table to a Specified Format — save_table
text: table_data data frame containing table data exported. Objects like model_fit, compare_model_fit, model_estimates supported. path string specifying file path table saved. file extension determines format (currently .docx supported). orientation character string indicating table's orientation using default template. Options "landscape" (default) "portrait". "vertical" accepted alias "portrait". Ignored custom template provided. template string specifying path custom Word template (.docx). NULL, default template provided package used, orientation set orientation argument. digits integer indicating number decimal places use formatting numeric columns. Defaults 3. ci_digits Number digits rounding confidence intervals. Default digits. p_digits Number digits rounding p-values. Default 3. digits_by_col Named integer vector forces digits selected columns. Applied export. model_fit compare_model_fit, defaults c(Chi2 = 2, Chi2_df = 2) supplied. Chi2_df fractional, rounded two decimals forming Chi2(df) header. table_args named list arguments forwarded prepare_table() ultimately insight::format_table().

title: Save a Table to a Specified Format — save_table
text: function checks file extension determine export format. Currently, Word documents (.docx) supported. function formats table using flextable package exports using officer package. Users can provide custom Word template rely package's default template, either "landscape" "portrait" orientation set orientation argument. path .docx extension, table saved Word document. default template aligns table APA style, Arial font size 10. custom template provided via template argument, takes precedence orientation setting.

title: Save a Table to a Specified Format — save_table
code: if (requireNamespace("lavaan", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(lavaan)   library(psymetrics)   hs_model <- 'visual  =~ x1 + x2 + x3                textual =~ x4 + x5 + x6                speed   =~ x7 + x8 + x9'   fit1 <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939, estimator = "ML")   fit2 <- cfa(hs_model, data = HolzingerSwineford1939, estimator = "MLR")   fit_compared <- compare_model_fit(fit1, fit2)   output_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".docx")   save_table(fit_compared, path = output_path, orientation = "landscape")   save_table(     fit_compared,     path = output_path,     orientation = "landscape",     digits_by_col = c(Chi2 = 2, SRMR = 2)   )   unlink(output_path) } else {   message("Please install 'lavaan' to run this example.") } #> Table successfully saved as a Word document at #> /tmp/Rtmp6WLsNa/file1a2578d47d8e.docx. #> Table successfully saved as a Word document at #> /tmp/Rtmp6WLsNa/file1a2578d47d8e.docx.

title: psymetrics 0.3.0
text: Parameter Extraction: Added model_estimates() lavaan CFA/SEM workflows, including optional standardized extraction (std., std.lv, std.nox) component filtering. Component Blocks: Parameter outputs organized semantic blocks (Loading, Regression, Correlation, Variance, Mean, Defined, Threshold, ) text/markdown/html/docx reporting. Formatting: Added format_results.model_estimates() print.model_estimates() integrate model_estimates package table pipeline. Exporting: save_table() now supports list-based table exports (used model_estimates) writes DOCX output per-component blocks.

title: psymetrics 0.3.0
text: Non-convergence: model_estimates.lavaan() now returns available parameter rows even fit converge, flags rows converged = FALSE, keeps unavailable inferential columns NA. Messaging & Warnings: verbose = TRUE, model_estimates() now reports non-convergence missing requested components; warnings emitted lavaan preserved propagated. Operator Coverage: Added explicit handling threshold unmapped operators, preserving rows component bucket instead dropping . Testing: Added dedicated tests extraction, component filtering, standardization aliases, non-converged (raw standardized) extraction, verbose messaging/warning propagation, formatting (text/markdown/html), printing, DOCX export.

title: psymetrics 0.2.0
text: SEM Support: Officially documented model_fit() compare_model_fit() support fitted lavaan SEM workflows (including CFA, SEM, growth, multigroup, multilevel variants). Testing: Expanded SEM coverage path-lavaan::sem() objects model_fit()/compare_model_fit(), added manual stress script (tests/manual/sem_stress_lavaan.R) broad SEM matrix checks. Model Fit / Compare Fit: test = NULL now treated "default" lavaan flows, clearer model-specific fallback messaging.

title: psymetrics 0.2.0
text: Plotting: plot_factor_loadings() now supports facet_by = c("none", "group", "level", "group_level") SEM contexts grouping metadata. Plotting (Breaking): plot_factor_loadings() now places verbose facet_by argument order. Positional (unnamed) calls may need updated; named arguments unaffected. Plotting: plot_factor_loadings() now errors fitted lavaan object measurement loadings (=~) instead returning silent empty plot. Plotting: plot_factor_loadings(verbose = FALSE) now suppresses non-fatal messages/warnings internal lavaan extraction calls, still raising errors. Plotting: plot_factor_loadings() now skips CI error bars CI values unavailable uses data-driven limits autofit = TRUE. Plotting: plot_factor_loadings() now uses ci (accepting legacy CI via ...), adds ci_bounds control standardized CI handling (extend vs arrow), warns non-converged models, refines autofit limits keep standardized bounds visible. Plotting: plot_factor_loadings() now maps standardized estimates column name (est.std) aborts explicit error estimate column available.

title: psymetrics 0.2.0
text: Model Fit: Non-converged lavaan fits now return single-row table converged = FALSE NA metrics, keeping compare_model_fit() stable. Model Fit: Non-convergence alerts now respect verbose include model labels available. Model Fit: Added model_fit.default() return clear error unsupported model classes.

title: psymetrics 0.2.0
text: Table Export: save_table() aligns default digits_by_col model_fit/compare_model_fit, accepts case-insensitive .docx, guards alignment single-column tables. Formatting: format_results() now accepts output_args$align top-level align supplied, errors passed, rejects output_args$format (use output). Formatting: Per-column numeric formatting now recognizes signed scientific-notation values (example 1e-05, -2E+03). Messaging: Standardized digits/ci_digits/p_digits conflict error English. Documentation: Corrected save_table() docs reflect default APA template font size (Arial 10).

title: psymetrics 0.1.9
text: Messaging: Robust-fit warnings now aggregate per test/estimator, missing-test messages include model label. Model Fit: Missing requested tests drop rows standard_test = FALSE, standard-models preserve standard SE extra standard row added. Compare Fit: Per-model test overrides omit models missing tests standard_test = FALSE. Compare Fit: Per-model overrides now accept named arguments unambiguous object names, clearer errors unknown duplicate model labels. Testing: Expanded model_fit/compare_model_fit coverage per-model test lists, missing tests, standard-fallback, robust warning aggregation.

title: psymetrics 0.1.8
text: Messaging: Suppressed standard-index fallback message test = "none" avoid redundant output returning NA metrics. API: model_fit.lavaan() adds test, standard_test, test_details control multi-test output; compare_model_fit() accepts arguments supports named lists override per-model selections. Model Fit: model_fit.lavaan() now reports one row per non-standard test multiple tests present; standard_test adds standard-test row first (always standard indices), output can include multiple rows per model. Model Fit: test_details = TRUE adds TEST SE columns retains _variant estimator labels technical output; standard_test = TRUE adds standard row, SE set NA fit indices depend standard errors. Model Fit: Missing robust fit measures now return NA warning instead erroring type = "robust" requested. Formatting: Chi-square df values model_fit compare_model_fit headers now round 2 decimals fractional.

title: psymetrics 0.1.7
text: lavaan 0.6.21 Compatibility: Updated model_fit.lavaan() handle multi-element options$test, align estimator detection current test/se defaults, correctly surface Browne residual defaults continuous ULS/DWLS fits (including clearer messaging scaled/robust indices unavailable). Estimator Identification: Expanded detection logic lavaan estimators options$test multi-element, covering robust ML variants WLS/ULS/DWLS cases Browne residual defaults. Testing: Added model_fit coverage Browne residual tests, Bollen-Stine bootstrap tests, test = "none", robust MLR/WLSMV estimator reporting.

title: psymetrics 0.1.6
text: Table Formatting Pipeline: prepare_table() now internal S3 generic focused producing base formatted data frame via insight::format_table(). New format_results(): Added S3 formatting helper returns text, markdown, HTML outputs table_args, output_args, digits_by_col, output = "auto" support. Print Methods: print.model_fit() print.compare_model_fit() now console-text outputs always return original object invisibly. save_table() Signature: save_table() now uses prepare_table() supports digits_by_col post-processing step, table_args forwards insight::format_table(). Removed: print_format(), emit, return_object, knitr..progress behavior print pipeline.

title: psymetrics 0.1.6
text: Integer Handling: Removed manual rounding columns_to_format handling, relying insight::format_table() integer-safe formatting.

title: psymetrics 0.1.6
text: File Organization: Refactored file layout separate backend-specific model_fit.* methods result-class methods, user-facing changes.

title: psymetrics 0.1.5
text: Dependency Management: Moved ggplot2 lavaan Imports Suggests minimize mandatory dependencies. Functions now provide informative prompts required package missing runtime. Robustness: Functions rely suggested packages now use internal checks (rlang::check_installed) provide clearer error messages dependencies missing. Examples: Updated examples run conditionally, prevents errors R CMD check provides smoother user experience. S3 Method Registration: Ensured correct robust S3 method registration plot print methods, guaranteeing proper method dispatch R. Testing: Added initial testthat suite covering model_fit, compare_model_fit, plotting, save_table, optional dependency checks. Plotting: Replaced deprecated geom_errorbarh() usage geom_errorbar() + orientation = "y" align current ggplot2 guidance. Table Export & Formatting: save_table() now accepts "portrait" (alias "vertical") template orientation forwards formatting arguments. prepare_table() respects ci_digits inputs. Documentation: Added central help page (?plot-methods) document generic plot function. Improved navigation help files @Seealso cross-references. Updated package Title DESCRIPTION better reflect purpose (“Unified Tools Psychometric Model Analysis”).

title: psymetrics 0.1.4
text: save_table(): new utility function saving tables generated model outputs different formats, starting Word (.docx). function automatically checks necessary packages (flextable officer) provides formatting tables accordance APA style. Key features: Default template landscape orientation. Options customize file path template. Digit precision control, options different font styles cell alignment. plot() method lavaan objects: Introduced plot method specifically designed lavaan objects, enabling users easily visualize key model results. Supported plots: Factor Loadings Plot (type = "loadings"): Displays standardized factor loadings CFA models options display confidence intervals adjust axis limits. Residuals Plot (type = "residuals") (future): display residuals model diagnostics evaluation. Path Diagram Plot (type = "path") (future): generate path diagrams SEM models. factor loadings plot includes parameters sorting grouping items, along flexible display options confidence intervals standardized estimates.

title: psymetrics 0.1.4
text: Internal Function Enhancements: Improved prepare_table function consistent table formatting across different outputs. includes automatic rounding character conversion key columns (NOBS, NPAR, Chi2_df) applicable. Improved Handling Global Variables: Adopted .data pronoun within ggplot2 avoid unnecessary global variable bindings improve compatibility R CMD check.

title: psymetrics 0.1.3
text: Enhanced Output Formats print.model_fit print.compare_model_fit: Added support exporting tables additional formats, including markdown html, using print.model_fit print.compare_model_fit functions. Customizable Alignment: Introduced align argument control table alignment text markdown formats. Available options include “left”, “right”, “center”, “firstleft”. details, refer insight::export_table documentation. Improved Error Handling: Functions now use cli::cli_abort error handling, providing clearer detailed messages unsupported parameters passed.

title: psymetrics 0.1.3
text: Code Refactoring: Made improvements structure print_format function related code, optimizing format handling code clarity. Documentation Update: documentation updated reflect new parameters features, including additional usage examples.

title: psymetrics 0.1.2
text: Added: New function compare_model_fit() allows comparing fit indices across multiple lavaan model objects. Usage: can pass multiple fitted lavaan model objects compare_model_fit() return data frame fit indices model, enabling easy comparison. Verbose Option: Added verbose argument control display informational messages comparison. Print Method: Implemented print.compare_model_fit method provide formatted output easy reading model comparisons.

title: psymetrics 0.1.2
text: Updated: Improved model_fit() function handle multiple types fit indices, including “standard”, “scaled”, “robust”. Custom Metrics: Introduced metrics argument, allowing users specify exactly fit indices want extract. metrics set “essential”, predefined set common indices returned. Verbose Option: Added verbose argument control display informational messages metrics adjusted according estimator type. Print Method: Implemented print.model_fit method provide clean formatted output fit indices, customizable precision.

title: psymetrics 0.1.2
text: Updated: Improved documentation model_fit() added comprehensive examples showcasing use compare_model_fit().

title: psymetrics 0.1.2
text: Tests Pending: Unit tests model_fit() compare_model_fit() added next update ensure consistent functionality output formatting.

title: psymetrics 0.1.1
text: output format can customized using digits, p_digits, format arguments. Automatically formats results using insight::format_table() outputs insight::export_table().

title: psymetrics 0.1.0
text: Supports three types fit indices: standard, scaled, robust. Allows users specify custom metrics using metrics argument. Provides informative messages regarding automatic adjustment metrics (scaled robust) based estimator used. model_fit function: Added generic function extracting model fit indices. Currently implemented objects class lavaan.

title: psymetrics 0.1.0
text: Fixed S3 method consistency issue model_fit model_fit.lavaan. Corrected declaration dependencies DESCRIPTION file include rlang Suggests.

title: psymetrics 0.1.0
text: NAMESPACE file now automatically managed using roxygen2 export appropriate functions maintain dependency order. Added check examples verify lavaan installed running .
